tasks.task: Extract all relevant topics for this content.
Take a deep breath and carefully review the information provided in the system prompt and this prompt.
Examine the given page, identifying all textual elements as defined in the XML tags.
Determine the primary reading direction and any additional languages present on the page.

For each identified text element: Assign a unique sequential ID (elem_001, elem_002, elem_003, etc.). Determine its type (e.g., h1, h2, paragraph, header, footer, sidebar, figure, footnote, caption). Assign an order number reflecting its position in the logical reading sequence. Extract the full text content of the element. Do not truncate, summarize, or paraphrase — every word matters for screen reader accessibility. For footnotes and captions, set the ref attribute to the ID of the element they reference. For figures, include both caption (visible text) and alt_text (description of visual content).
Pay special attention to the layout of the page, including columns, sidebars, and any non-standard text arrangements.

Determine heading hierarchy with care — this drives the entire document structure downstream: Identify the main title or most prominent heading as h1. There is typically one h1 per document (or per page for standalone pages). Identify major section headings as h2. These divide the document into main parts. Identify subsection headings as h3 or h4. Use visual prominence (size, weight, spacing) as a guide, but verify against document structure. A visually smaller heading that opens a new major section should be h2, not h3. Do not skip heading levels. If you see what appears to be h1 followed by h3 with no h2, verify this is correct. If uncertain, prefer the higher level.
Ensure that footnotes, captions, and other supplementary text are extracted in a logical order relative to their references in the main text.
For complex layouts, mentally map out the reading flow before beginning the extraction process.
If you encounter unusual text arrangements or are unsure of the correct reading order, refer to the guidance in the XML tags.

Organize your extracted text according to the structure provided in the XML tags, ensuring that: Every element has a unique id attribute. Every element has an order attribute reflecting reading sequence. Order numbers are sequential with no gaps (1, 2, 3, not 1, 3, 5). Cross-references use the ref attribute where applicable. Heading levels follow a logical hierarchy without skipping levels.

Review your extracted text to ensure completeness, accuracy, and proper ordering: Verify you have captured all textual content from the page. Confirm paragraph text is complete — no sentences cut off, no words missing. Verify heading levels form a valid hierarchy (h1 → h2 → h3, no skips). Confirm every footnote has a ref pointing to its source element. Confirm every figure has both caption and alt_text.
Take a final deep breath and ensure your extraction is thorough, follows all provided guidelines, and accurately represents the full textual content of the page in its original reading order.
